creator: thefloridaqueenie
caption:
Looking for things to do around Florida? Comment "Guide" and I'll send you my interactive Florida map and guide with over 1,000 spots to explore! I did the work for you so you can just hit the road without hours of research and stress!

Boca Grande is the ultimate bucket list destination and most beautiful island town in Florida in my opinion!

Be sure to check out the Gasparilla Inn and Club!

Get a lobster roll at Newlin’s Gourmet!

 Get an ice cream cone at the Pink Pony! 

#bocagrandeflorida #gasparillaisland #floridabeachtowns #uniqueflorida #hiddengemsflorida

transcription.text: You can stop searching for the most incredible Florida island town because I found it for you. Welcome to Boca Grande, located on Gasparilla Island on the west coast of Florida, about two hours south of Tampa. This place is a true bucket list destination. Boca Grande is one of my favorite island towns because it's not only beautiful and unspoiled, but there is also so much to see and do here. You won't find any high-rises, chain restaurants, nightclubs, or traffic lights, and I'm not mad about it. I love coming here for day trips, so this is what I suggest you do. While you can totally explore by car, a popular option is renting a golf cart or bicycle to get around this small island, which is only 7 miles long and 1 mile wide. Visit the Gasparilla Inn and Club, which was built in 1911 and is the most iconic hotel on the island. I definitely felt like I was walking into a bygone era while visiting here, and I've never seen so many orchids that weren't in a botanical garden in one place. This place is dripping with old Florida charm, so much so that there's a dress code. Don't worry, you can visit here, dress casually, but if you want to have afternoon tea or a meal at their on-site restaurant, be sure to dress nice. They also offer a historic tour of the hotel, a culinary tour of the kitchen, and a botanical walking tour of the grounds. Pro tip, keep an eye out for celebrities. Go to Newland's Gourmet for the best lobster roll anywhere, including Maine. And yes, I've been to Maine. This is better. Get an ice cream cone at the legendary Pink Pony and explore the downtown area. There are so many beautiful, eclectic shops and great restaurants and cafes. Keep an eye out.
